Figma Visual Grounding — Experiment Output

Module 7 · Equal Groups · 11 beats · 5 frames fetched · 4 edited by FLUX.2-Klein

dialogue

Hi! Today we're going to learn about something called equal groups. Get ready — you're going to see groups of things, and we're going to figure out what makes them special!

scene · add · equal_groups
source frame
edited for scene
{
  "groups": 3,
  "items_per_group": 4,
  "grouping_type": "bags",
  "item_type": "pictures",
  "item_subtype": "oranges",
  "arrangement": "2x2",
  "orientation": "horizontal",
  "mode": 1,
  "highlight_state": "none",
  "labels_visible": false,
  "mc_options_visible": false
}
figma_ref: counting-group-total-on-screen
dialogue

Look at these bags. Each bag holds some oranges. How many oranges do you think are in each bag? Let's find out!

scene · animate · equal_groups
source frame
edited for scene
{
  "groups": 3,
  "items_per_group": 4,
  "grouping_type": "bags",
  "item_type": "pictures",
  "item_subtype": "oranges",
  "arrangement": "2x2",
  "orientation": "horizontal",
  "mode": 1,
  "animation": "sequential_item_highlight",
  "animation_target_group": 1,
  "highlight_state": "group_1_active",
  "labels_visible": true,
  "label_values": [
    4,
    null,
    null
  ],
  "mc_options_visible": false
}
figma_ref: counting-group-total-on-screen
dialogue

There are 4 oranges in that bag! Now watch — each bag has 4 oranges. Every single bag has the same amount. That's what we call equal groups — groups that all have the same number of things!

scene · animate · equal_groups
source frame
edited for scene
{
  "groups": 3,
  "items_per_group": 4,
  "grouping_type": "bags",

  "arrangement": "2x2",
  "orientation": "horizontal",
  "mode": 1,
  "animation": "all_groups_highlight_simultaneous",
  "highlight_state": "all_active",
  "labels_visible": true,
  "label_values": [
    4,
    4,
    4
  ],
  "mc_options_visible": false
}
figma_ref: counting-group-total-on-screen
prompt

figma_ref: 12x5-semi-abstract-groups
dialogue

Amazing work! Remember: equal groups means every group has the same number of items. We counted the groups, and we saw that every circle had exactly 5 dots. In the lessons ahead, we'll start using these groups to multiply. You're already thinking like a mathematician!
